agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Sprint 25 / Session 165 -- US-291 SHIPPED passes:true (B-044 lint exempt: seed_eclipse_vin.py 3 hardcoded addresses). Pre-flight audit confirmed Rex inbox-note diagnosis: tests/lint/test_no_hardcoded_addresses.py::TestAuditRepositoryStandingRule::test_auditRepository_cleanRepository_zeroFindings reports exactly 3 violations matching the spec line-numbers (line 80 hostname 'chi-eclipse-01' / line 332 ip 'http://10.27.27.10:8000' / line 333 hostname 'chi-srv-01'). Marker convention discovered via `rg '# b044-exempt:'`: same-line `# b044-exempt: <reason>` AFTER the literal (per scanLine pragma parser at test_no_hardcoded_addresses.py:81). 11 prior precedents in scripts/ + src/ + deploy/ inform reason-text shape. Implementation: scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py line 80 gained 'canonical Eclipse seeder; device id frozen as version-controlled truth' (mirrors the file's own top-comment header at line 76); line 332 gained 'argparse default; production chi-srv-01 fallback for one-off Eclipse seeder'; line 333 gained 'argparse help prose' (matches existing argparse-help precedent in scripts/sync_now.py:127, scripts/report.py:160, scripts/record_drain_test.py:133). Modification History block updated with US-291 entry (2026-05-07 Rex). Comment-only change -- zero functional behavior delta. Pre-fix RED: targeted test FAILED with 3 violations; post-fix GREEN: 1 passed in 23.96s. Full file suite 16/16 GREEN (83.64s). Sprint_lint clean (0 errors / 0 warnings across 8 stories). Ruff drift observed: scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py:237-238 uses `from datetime import datetime, timezone` + `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` instead of `datetime.UTC` alias (UP017/UP004). 2 ruff errors PRE-EXISTING since 2026-05-01 commit 35e1374 (verified via `git show HEAD:scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py`). Out of US-291 scope per Refusal Rule 3. PM may file as TD or address in cleanup pass. RECOVERY NOTE: this session attempted `git stash` mid-flight to verify ruff baseline -- aborted because offices/ralph/ralph.sh held a permission-denied lock from the parent ralph.sh process. Working tree was partially-reverted; recovered via `git checkout stash@{0} -- <30-file-list-excluding-ralph.sh>`. stash@{0} remains in the repo as redundant safety copy; PM can `git stash drop stash@{0}` at sprint close. ralph.sh itself is unchanged; only the locked-file artifact is the residue. Sprint 25 closure: US-284 through US-291 ALL passes:true. PM action at sprint close: stage all unstaged changes, commit per conventional-commit format, tag V0.25.0, deploy server + Pi per closeout-pm skill; drop stash@{0} as cleanup. Standing by; Sprint 25 COMPLETE. Prior session note: Sprint 25 / Session 164 -- US-290 SHIPPED passes:true (TD-007 close: OLLAMA_GENERATE_TIMEOUT configurable; open since 2026-02-05). Pre-flight audit (rg OLLAMA_GENERATE_TIMEOUT|aiAnalysis|generateTimeoutSeconds src/ src/common/config/ config.json) found two scope drifts vs story spec: (1) Phantom-path drift: scope used legacy aiAnalysis.* path but current shape is server.ai.* per loader.py:124 rename comment + config.json layout -- per stopCondition[1] reconciled to current shape. (2) Consumer-surface drift: scope listed analyzer.py as consumer but the actual urlopen(timeout=) call site is analyzer_ollama.py:97 (callOllama function) -- per stopCondition[0] extended scope to include analyzer_ollama.py with new timeoutSeconds kwarg matching the existing callOllamaChat parameter shape (US-CMP-005 origin). Same precedent class as US-287/US-289 phantom-path-drift handling. Implementation: validator.DEFAULTS gained 'server.ai.generateTimeoutSeconds': 120 (DEFAULTS-dict idiom, stronger than US-OLL-002 sibling pattern which only does read-with-fallback); config.json gained 'generateTimeoutSeconds: 120' under server.ai (next to apiTimeoutSeconds: 60 + healthTimeoutSeconds: 10); types.py OLLAMA_GENERATE_TIMEOUT recommented as default-fallback; analyzer.py __init__ reads aiConfig.get('generateTimeoutSeconds', OLLAMA_GENERATE_TIMEOUT) into self._generateTimeoutSeconds and threads it via _callOllama -> callOllama(timeoutSeconds=...); analyzer_ollama.callOllama gained `timeoutSeconds: int = OLLAMA_GENERATE_TIMEOUT` parameter applied at urlopen(request, timeout=timeoutSeconds). Test path drift: spec said tests/ai/ but current layout is tests/server/test_*.py flat -- landed at tests/server/test_ollama_generate_timeout_configurable.py (6 tests across 3 classes: TestAiAnalyzerReadsTimeoutFromConfig + TestCallOllamaAcceptsTimeoutParam + TestValidatorDefaultsHasGenerateTimeoutKey). Pre-fix RED: 5 fail (1 guardrail-pass = fallback-equals-constant, naturally passes both pre/post); post-fix all 6 GREEN. 109 sibling tests (test_config_validator + tests/server/test_analyze* + test_report_ai + test_remote_ollama) green; ruff clean across all 5 touched files; sprint_lint clean (0 errors / 0 warnings across 8 stories); --check-feedback flags US-284-289 standing PM-staging pattern, US-290 itself OK. validate_config.py confirms config.json valid post-edit. PM action: TD-007 archive move with closure date 2026-05-07 + commit hash. Sprint 25: P0/P1/P2 (US-284-290) all closed; only US-291 (P3 lint exempt) remains. Standing by post-US-290. Prior session note: Sprint 25 / Session 163 -- US-289 SHIPPED passes:true (Spool Story 6: battery_health_log column rename to start_vcell_v / end_vcell_v). Pre-flight audit confirmed Spool's diagnosis: orchestrator.py:861-866 + 952-955 pass VCELL volts (3.4-4.2V) into startSoc/endSoc parameters; legacy column comment claimed '0..100 (MAX17048 integer % scale)' -- four-sprint-old documentation lie. Schema lives in src/pi/power/battery_health.py (NOT src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py as listed in scope.filesToTouch -- phantom-path drift documented in completionNotes; same precedent as US-287 Session 161). Implementation: SCHEMA_BATTERY_HEALTH_LOG gained start_vcell_v + end_vcell_v REAL nullable columns with deprecated comments on legacy start_soc/end_soc; new ensureBatteryHealthLogVcellColumns(conn) idempotent migration helper mirrors US-252 ensurePowerLogVcellColumn (PRAGMA table_info probe before each ALTER; returns True if at least one ALTER ran); BatteryHealthRecorder.startDrainEvent + endDrainEvent INSERT/UPDATE BOTH legacy *_soc and new *_vcell_v columns with the same VCELL value per stopCondition[1] deprecation contract. Wired the migration helper into ObdDatabase.initialize() in src/pi/obdii/database.py (out-of-scope but required per AC 5; same pattern as US-252's ensurePowerLogVcellColumn wiring). New test file tests/pi/power/test_battery_health_log_columns.py (15 tests / 4 classes): TestNewColumnsPresentAfterInitialize / TestEnsureBatteryHealthLogVcellColumnsIdempotency (incl. legacy-rows-preserved test) / TestRecorderEmitsToBothColumns (incl. close-once preserved for both pairs) / TestEnsureChainComposability. Pre-fix all 15 FAILED with ImportError on missing symbol (red phase verified); post-fix all 15 GREEN. Updated tests/pi/data/test_battery_health_log_schema.py::test_columnShape strict-equality expected-set to include the 2 new column names (out-of-scope deviation; documented). Verification: 48 battery_health tests GREEN in 62s; full fast suite GREEN modulo the standing US-291 B-044 lint RED (pre-existing since 2026-05-01, NOT a US-289 regression); ruff clean on touched files; sprint_lint clean (0 errors / 0 warnings across 8 stories). Sprint 25: P0/P1 (US-284-287) + P2 (US-288/289) closed; P2/P3 remain (US-290/291). Standing by post-US-289. Prior session note: Sprint 25 / Session 162 -- US-288 SHIPPED passes:true (Spool Story 5: stage state-machine latching). Pre-flight audit found existing _state-based escalation gates re-fire on every tick that satisfies threshold AFTER a hysteresis-induced de-escalation back to NORMAL -- the bug class behind Spool's 7 WARNING + 6 IMMINENT vs 4 TRIGGER row count discrepancy. Fix: new _highWaterStage: PowerState attribute (init NORMAL) set by _enterWarning -> WARNING, _enterImminent -> IMMINENT, _enterTrigger -> TRIGGER; reset to NORMAL by _acRestore. Escalation gates replaced: WARNING fires only if _highWaterStage == NORMAL; IMMINENT only if _highWaterStage in (NORMAL, WARNING); TRIGGER only if _highWaterStage in (NORMAL, WARNING, IMMINENT). 6 new tests in tests/pi/power/test_stage_latching.py across 3 classes -- 3 discriminators (FAILS pre-fix, PASSES post-fix); 3 guardrails. tests/pi/power/ (124 tests) all green incl. US-234 hysteresis tests; ruff clean; sprint_lint clean. Hysteresis 0.05V invariant preserved -- latching gate orthogonal to hysteresis (de-escalation mutates _state but NOT _highWaterStage). TRIGGER atomicity preserved -- new high-water gate is additional safety atop existing _state==TRIGGER terminal early-return + _shutdownFired flag. Drain-Test-11 row-count verification is POST-SPRINT-25 CIO bench action. Sprint 25: P0/P1 (US-284-287) + P2 (US-288) closed; P2/P3 remain (US-289/290/291). Standing by post-US-288. Prior session note: Sprint 25 / Session 161 -- US-287 SHIPPED passes:true (Spool Story 4: startup_log writer wiring). Pre-flight audit found the WRITER already existed (US-263 shipped writeStartupLog + recordBootReason; US-283 schema-pinned 5 cols) -- only the call site was missing. New LifecycleMixin._recordStartupLog method in src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py wires recordBootReason(self._database) at the earliest DB-access site: right after _initializeDatabase and BEFORE _initializeProfileManager / _initializeConnection -- crucially before the US-244/US-284 connect-blocker territory so the row lands every boot regardless of OBD outcome. New TestLifecycleWiring class (5 tests in tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py): method-exists / invokes-with-database / swallows-exception / database-None-noop / source-level call-order assertion. All 5 fail pre-fix (red phase verified); all pass post-fix. 52/52 boot_reason tests + 100/100 orchestrator tests pass; fast suite has 1 documented failure (US-291 B-044 baseline). ruff clean; sprint_lint clean. Wiring scope deviation: scope.filesToTouch lists only boot_reason.py + test_boot_reason.py, not lifecycle.py; per stopConditions[0] ('pick the earliest site that has DB access + document choice in completionNotes') touching lifecycle.py is explicitly authorized -- documented in completionNotes. Module-level import uses no-prefix `from pi.diagnostics.boot_reason import recordBootReason` matching existing lifecycle.py shape (avoids V0.24.1 cross-module dual-resolution anti-pattern). Drain Test 11 prior_boot_clean=true confirmation is POST-SPRINT-25 CIO bench action item (honesty disclosure). With US-284/285/286/287 SHIPPED, Sprint 25 P0+P1 closed; P2-P3 remain (US-288/289/290/291). Standing by post-US-287.

agents.name: Agent2
agents.note: Sprint 25 / Session 158 -- US-286 SHIPPED passes:true. tests/pi/integration/test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py (1 class / 1 test / 7 phases): real ApplicationOrchestrator + DriveDetector + SummaryRecorder + ObdDataLogger; mock surface = connection.obd.query (BT edge) only; 0.16s call duration vs 30-sec budget. Mirrors US-260 cold-start pattern. Discriminator: would FAIL against May-4-5 production regression class (DriveDetector never operational) + against any silent realtime_data write regression. Two gotchas surfaced for future tests: (1) e2e fixtures must seed profiles('daily') because realtime_data has FK + bare orchestrator doesn't run ProfileManager; (2) production order query->logReading->_handleReading means first N ticks land with drive_id NULL -- query realtime_data without drive_id filter. Standing by post-US-286.
